claims.verification_note: packages/zod/package.json shows "version": "4.4.3" and versions.ts confirms major:4 minor:4 patch:3. Walkthrough says '4.x' which is correct as a range.

claims.claim_summary: Project structure: packages/zod/src/v4/{core,classic,locales,mini}/, packages/zod/src/v3/, packages/bench/, packages/docs/, packages/integration/
claims.verification_note: Verified: v4/core/, v4/classic/, v4/locales/, v4/mini/, v3/, bench/, docs/, integration/ all exist under the expected paths.

claims.claim_summary: Core package has zero runtime dependencies
claims.verification_note: packages/zod/package.json has no 'dependencies' or 'devDependencies' field. All dependencies are in the root package.json as devDependencies. Confirmed zero runtime deps.

claims.claim_summary: $ZodType base class defined in v4/core/core.ts with _zod property containing def and traits
claims.verification_note: core.ts defines $constructor with _zod property containing def, constr, and traits. The walkthrough's simplified interface (showing _zid, _def, parse, safeParse, optional, nullable) is a conceptual illustration, not literal code. Actual implementation uses _zod.def and _zod.traits.

claims.claim_summary: Classic parse.ts provides parse, safeParse, parseAsync, safeParseAsync functions
claims.verification_note: classic/parse.ts exports parse (line 8), parseAsync (line 15), safeParse (line 22), safeParseAsync (line 29). The walkthrough signatures are simplified; actual signatures use core.$ZodType and additional params.

claims.claim_summary: $ZodIssue interface has code, message, path, expected, received fields
claims.verification_note: errors.ts defines $ZodIssueBase with code, input, path, message fields. $ZodIssueInvalidType adds expected field. 'received' is not a direct field but implied via input. Simplified for clarity.

claims.claim_summary: v4/locales/ contains en.ts, zh-CN.ts, ja.ts locale files
claims.verification_note: Confirmed: en.ts, zh-CN.ts, ja.ts all exist in v4/locales/. There are 50+ locale files total (ar, az, be, bg, ca, cs, da, de, el, en, eo, es, fa, fi, fr, fr-CA, he, hr, hu, hy, id, is, it, ja, ka, kh, km, ko, lt, mk, ms, nl, no, ota, pl, ps, pt, ro, ru, sl, sv, ta, th, tr, ua, uk, ur, uz, vi, yo, zh-CN, zh-TW).

claims.claim_summary: Classic layer files: string.ts, number.ts, boolean.ts, object.ts, array.ts, union.ts, record.ts, tuple.ts, enum.ts, native-enum.ts, effects.ts, coerce.ts, brand.ts, parse.ts, error.ts, external.ts
claims.verification_note: INCORRECT: The walkthrough lists individual type files (string.ts, number.ts, etc.) but the actual v4/classic/ directory has a different structure: checks.ts, coerce.ts, compat.ts, errors.ts, external.ts, from-json-schema.ts, index.ts, iso.ts, parse.ts, schemas.ts. Types are consolidated into schemas.ts and checks.ts, not split into individual files. The walkthrough's file listing does not match the actual code.

claims.claim_summary: Core layer exports $ZodType, $constructor, $ZodError, $ZodIssue, parse, safeParse from v4/core/
claims.verification_note: v4/core/index.ts re-exports from core.js, parse.js, errors.js, schemas.js. Simplified but accurate.

claims.claim_summary: Zod uses lazy method binding (getters) for optional/nullable etc. to reduce instance creation overhead
claims.verification_note: schemas.ts lines 10-18 document the lazy-bind mechanism: builder methods live as non-enumerable getters on prototypes, allocated on first access and cached as own properties. The walkthrough's description of lazy binding is accurate.
Root package.json devDependencies includes "esbuild": "^0.25.5" and "rollup": "^4.60.2". Also includes "rolldown": "1.0.0-beta.18" and "tsdown": "^0.21.10" as build tools.

claims.claim_summary: v4 has built-in JSON Schema conversion via z.toJsonSchema() and z.fromJsonSchema()
claims.verification_note: external.ts exports toJSONSchema from json-schema-processors.js and fromJSONSchema from from-json-schema.ts. Confirmed both directions of conversion exist.
